frameworks.name: renormalization group
frameworks.one_line: iterative coarse-graining to expose macroscopic simplifications of microscopic neural interactions

frameworks.name: criticality
frameworks.one_line: power-law scaling and non-trivial fixed point as signatures of collective network behavior

core_question: Can renormalization-group-style coarse-graining reveal a non-trivial fixed point in the collective activity of large neural populations?
key_assumptions: macroscopic neural behavior may admit simpler descriptions than microscopic interactions
RG ideas from physics can be adapted to biological neural population data
manipulations: coarse-graining scale varied across roughly two decades
construction of explicit coarse-graining procedures applied to recorded population activity
key_findings: coarse-grained variable distributions approach a fixed non-Gaussian form
power-law dependencies emerge in both static and dynamic quantities across scales
interpretations: collective network activity is described by a non-trivial fixed point
RG-style simplification can succeed for complex biological neural systems
